key_insights.insight: Most people in the Western world will die from mismatched diseases, which are conditions our bodies did not evolve to handle well, like chronic stress and unhealthy diets.
key_insights.quote: The vast majority of us in the Western world will die from a mismatched disease.

key_insights.insight: Hunter-gatherers can be a good starting point for emulating certain practices, but are not ideal role models in every aspect of health because their primary adaptation was for reproductive success.
key_insights.quote: There are plenty of things that they do that are worth emulating, but they're not role models in every respect.

key_insights.insight: Humans are the ultimate omnivores, able to adapt to a wide range of diets and environments through technology and a flexible digestive system.
key_insights.quote: The fact of the matter is that we evolved to eat just about everything. We are the ultimate omnivores.

key_insights.insight: Big brains and fat bodies are intimately connected; fat is essential for energy storage, especially for infants' brain development and women's reproductive capabilities.
key_insights.quote: So big brains and fat bodies are intimately connected. So we want to make sure our babies are fat. A fat baby is an essential fundamental human adaptation.

key_insights.insight: When dieting, the body goes into a 'starvation response', elevating cortisol levels and increasing hunger and belly fat storage.
key_insights.quote: When you go into negative energy balance, which is what a diet is... Your body goes into a starvation response. Your cortisol levels go up, for example, right?

key_insights.insight: Chronic psychosocial stress, such as racism or commuting, has negative health effects by raising cortisol levels and directing fat storage to visceral deposits, which cause inflammation.
key_insights.quote: Psychosocial stress has pernicious effects...commuting, have negative health consequences because it causes our cortisol levels to go up. It causes us to put fat in the wrong places.

key_insights.insight: A sanitized environment can prevent immune systems from being properly challenged, leading to allergies and autoimmune diseases.
key_insights.quote: Now in this highly sanitized world, I still have the same immune system, but now it's like, it's like, doesn't have anything to do, right?

key_insights.insight: Cultural evolution has drastically outpaced biological evolution, leading to novel environments and mismatched diseases. Treating the symptoms of mismatched diseases exacerbates the fundamental problem.
key_insights.quote: Cultural evolution is so powerful and so rapid. It's so fast. It's so transformative that we have made our world so vastly and rapidly different that our bodies cannot possibly keep up in terms of our biology.

actionable_advice.advice: Interrupt sitting bouts by getting up every 20 minutes to activate metabolism and muscles.
actionable_advice.how_to: Set a timer for 20 minutes while sitting, and when it goes off, stand up, walk around, stretch, or do a quick task before sitting down again.

actionable_advice.advice: Be skeptical of health products and advice, as many offer little benefit or have unintended consequences.
actionable_advice.how_to: Research claims thoroughly, look for scientific evidence, and consider potential downsides before adopting new products or practices.

actionable_advice.advice: Prioritize regular physical activity to build a healthy skeletal system, especially during youth.
actionable_advice.how_to: Engage in weight-bearing exercises and encourage children to participate in at least an hour of physical activity daily.

actionable_advice.advice: Focus on treating the causes of health problems instead of just the symptoms to address the underlying issues that contribute to mismatched diseases.
actionable_advice.how_to: Consult with healthcare professionals to identify the root causes of health concerns and develop holistic treatment plans that incorporate lifestyle modifications.

memorable_stories: Davina McCall, a famous UK TV presenter, called the podcast host at 7 AM while running down the street after listening to the first conversation with Daniel Lieberman
The Turkana boy fossil discovery showed a shift in human bodies towards bipedalism and a more human-like form, coinciding with the rise of hunting and gathering.
Bangladeshi women who moved to England saw a significant increase in their cancer rates, illustrating the impact of energy-rich diets and reduced physical activity on disease.
contrarian_claims: Nasal breathing is not necessarily superior and elite runners breathe out through their mouth to cool the body.
We shouldn't just look at hunter gatherers as role models on every front because the primary function of their adaptions were for reproductive success
Dieting triggers a starvation response, increasing cortisol and making the body resist weight loss.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - World's No. 1 Exercise Professor: "Our Comfortable Lives Are Causing Cancer", "The Truth About Running", "Hand Sanitiser Is Making You Sick!
